data.narrative: The module developed strong understanding of container reproducibility, Docker layer caching, and production-safe container practices. The student successfully containerized a FastAPI service, diagnosed dependency and build-context failures, and validated runtime health and non-root execution inside Docker. The strongest performance area was failure reasoning and understanding why reproducible runtime environments matter in production systems.

dimension_scores.observation: Successfully implemented a multi-stage Docker build with health checks and non-root execution.
dimension_scores.evidence: Built and verified a FastAPI service container using Debian Slim, multi-stage builds, HEALTHCHECK, and appuser runtime execution.

dimension_scores.observation: Understood Docker layer ordering and cache invalidation behavior.
dimension_scores.evidence: Explained that placing COPY . . before dependency installation forces dependency reinstall on every source change.

dimension_scores.observation: Added and verified runtime health endpoints for service monitoring.
dimension_scores.evidence: Implemented and tested /live and /ready endpoints successfully through Docker networking.

dimension_scores.observation: Diagnosed dependency drift, build-context contamination, and platform-specific container failures.
dimension_scores.evidence: Resolved tensorflow-intel Linux incompatibility and identified .dockerignore omissions as a source of slow builds and leaked files.

dimension_scores.observation: Demonstrated awareness of runtime isolation and production recovery concerns.
dimension_scores.evidence: Connected large image sizes to slower deployment, autoscaling, and recovery behavior.

dimension_scores.observation: Provided concise explanations of Docker caching, runtime reproducibility, and security tradeoffs.
dimension_scores.evidence: Clearly explained layer invalidation, multi-stage builds, and non-root execution benefits.

strengths: Strong understanding of reproducible deployment environments and dependency isolation.
Correctly diagnosed build-context contamination and Docker caching regressions.
Successfully verified runtime health, networking, and non-root execution inside the container.
data.recommendation: In the next module, focus on automating build, verification, and deployment workflows through CI/CD pipelines.

public_evidence.student_action: Built and verified a multi-stage production container
public_evidence.technical_detail: Dockerfile, HEALTHCHECK, appuser runtime, /live and /ready endpoints
public_evidence.reviewer_value: Demonstrates understanding of reproducible deployments and container security

concepts_demonstrated.evidence: Explained why COPY ordering affects dependency reinstall behavior.
Explained why containers provide consistent runtime environments across systems.
Verified runtime execution as appuser instead of root.

teaching_approaches.detail: Used rebuild slowdown behavior to explain cache invalidation.
Used dependency failures between Windows and Linux environments to reinforce reproducibility concepts.
